data.title: Trump mineral plan will create bigger problems
data.description:
The US wants to produce more metals like rare earth and apply tariffs on Chinese imports. Hiking production in the $325 bln market makes sense but opening new mines will take decades. In the meantime, Washington will have to scramble for limited supplies from other countries.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @karenkkwok on X

CONTEXT NEWS

U.S. President Donald Trump on April 15 ordered a probe into potential new tariffs on all U.S. critical minerals imports.

Trump signed an order directing Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick to begin a national security review under Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962. That is the same law Trump used in his first term to impose 25% global tariffs on steel and aluminium and the one he used in February to launch a probe into copper tariffs.

Within 180 days, Lutnick is required to report his findings to the president, including whether to impose tariffs.

The review will assess U.S. vulnerabilities for the processing of all critical minerals - including cobalt, nickel and the 17 rare earths, as well as uranium - how foreign actors could be distorting markets, and what steps could be taken to boost domestic supply and recycling, according to the order.

data.title: Intel rises on report of foundry chip contract with Microsoft
data.description:
** Shares of chipmaker Intel rise 3.2% to $21 premarket

** Intel has reportedly entered into a large-scale foundry contract with software giant Microsoft using its 18A process, South Korea's Chosun Biz reported

** Tech heavyweights Google and Nvidia are also in talks - report

** Intel's 18A process is facing "ups and downs," the company said last month and high-volume production on 18A will begin towards the second-half of 2025

** Reuters reported in March that Broadcom and Nvidia have run tests on Intel's 18A

** Up to last close, Intel had risen 1.3% YTD

data.title: Tong Yang Industry Logs NT$1.47 Billion Profit in Q1
data.description:
Tong Yang Industry posted a profit attributable to owners of the parent of NT$1.47 billion or NT$2.48 per basic share in the first quarter.

The automotive and motorcycle parts manufacturer did not provide comparative year-ago figures.

Operating revenue accumulated for the year stood at NT$7.29 billion, according to a Wednesday Taiwan Exchange filing.

data.title: Kromek Group CFO to Retire in June; Successor Named
data.description:
Kromek Group appointed Claire Burgess as chief financial officer to succeed Paul Farquhar, who is retiring, effective June 1.

Burgess will join the radiation and bio-detection technology company on May 12 to ensure a smooth handover period, according to a Thursday filing.

data.title: Zoo Digital Group Names New Finance Chief
data.description:
Zoo Digital Group said Thursday it named Robert Pursell as its new chief financial officer.

The UK-listed digital media services company expects the new finance chief to assume the role and join the board on or before Aug. 8.

Outgoing CFO Phillip Blundell's decision to leave the group was announced in January.

data.title: Hong Kong Foreign Reserves Hit Lowest Since 2017
data.description:
Foreign exchange reserves in Hong Kong eased to $408.7 billion in April, marking the lowest level since July 2017, down from $412.5 billion in the previous month.

The amount represented more than five times the currency in circulation, or about 36% of Hong Kong dollar M3.

Including unsettled foreign exchange contracts, the foreign currency reserve assets amounted to $407.9 billion.

data.title: Himax Technologies: Div Equivalent to 18.5c/Shr >HIMX
Bitcoin Price Nears $100,000. What Could Get the Crypto There.
data.description:
By Elsa Ohlen

Bitcoin was nearing the psychological important level of $100,000 for the first time in three months as the first trade deal, since President Donald Trump's Liberation Day tariffs announcements, appeared imminent.

The world's largest crypto is up 2.3% to $99,284 over the last 24 hours, according to CoinDesk data.

Ether rose 4.2% and XRP gained 1.9% while futures tracking the S&P 500 were up 0.8%.

Trump is expected to announce a trade deal framework with the U.K. Thursday, The Wall Street Journal reported Trump teased the announcement in a post on Truth Social, saying it concerns a "major trade deal with representatives of a big, and highly respected country." He said a news conference would take place Thursday at 10.00 Eastern time.

Cryptos typically respond to macro events in the same way as other risk assets do. They drop when the economic climate sours or during times of increased market uncertainty, and vice versa. High tariffs could hit corporate profits and increase inflation, and the lack of clarity around the size of the levies has got investors nervous.

Keeping interest rates steady as expected Wednesday afternoon, Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell cited "extremely elevated" uncertainty around the economy and the impact of potential reciprocal tariffs.

Clarity around tariffs, be it through trade deals or de-escalation with China, could push Bitcoin to $100,000 for the first time since early February.

Write to Elsa Ohlen at elsa.ohlen@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: WT Microelectronics Logs Q1 Profit of NT$2.71 Billion
data.description:
WT Microelectronics posted a profit attributable to owners of NT$2.71 billion, or NT$2.42 per basic share, for the first quarter, according to a Thursday Taiwan bourse filing.

The company did not provide comparative year-ago figures.

Operating revenue stood at NT$247.4 billion, the integrated circuit (IC) design company said.

data.title: Philippines' GDP Eases With 5.4% Growth in Q1
data.description:
The Philippines' economic growth slowed down in the first quarter as US tariffs brought pressure on exports and investments.

The country's gross domestic product grew 5.4% during the quarter, according to data the Philippine Statistics Authority published on Thursday.

The current figures were higher than the 5.3% growth in the previous quarter, but it was slower than the 5.9% expansion during the first quarter of 2024. It also did not meet the 5.7% rise predicted by analysts polled by Reuters.

"We ranked second among our Asian peers that have already released their first quarter figures," the Department of Economy, Planning, and Development's secretary, Arsenio Balisacan, said in a speech delivered by Undersecretary Rosemarie Edillon, "this performance underscores the relative resilience of our economy in the face of global volatility."

The PSA's data showed that all major economic sectors, such as agriculture, forestry and fishing, and services, grew during the quarter despite the markets being disrupted by the tariffs announced by the US.

ING's regional head of research for Asia-Pacific, Deepali Bhargava, said the growth of private consumption and government spending was strong ahead of the upcoming elections on May 12, but the growth in net export and investment showed a disappointing performance.

"This highlights uncertainty around business confidence amid tariff escalations and a slowdown in trade," Bhargava said. "This suggests there was less frontloading of exports in 1Q ahead of the implementation of tariffs, a dynamic that's more evident in other export-driven economies."

ING reduced its 2025 GDP outlook for the Philippines to 5.6% year on year.

data.title: MPCC: Minutes from the Annual General Meeting 2025
data.description:
Oslo, Norway, 8 May 2025 - The annual general meeting of MPC Container Ships ASA (the "Company") was held today as a virtual meeting with online participation.

The minutes from the meeting are enclosed to this release and available on the Company's website.

data.title: Toluene prices down adjust in Europe
data.description: On Wednesday, toluene prices declined in Europe.An industry source in Europe informed a Polymerupdate team member,

data.title: Tripod Technology Logs Q1 Profit of NT$2.36 Billion
data.description:
Tripod Technology posted a profit attributable to owners of the parent of NT$2.36 billion, or NT$4.48 per basic share, for the first quarter, according to a Thursday Taiwan bourse filing.

The company did not provide comparative year-ago figures.

Operating revenue stood at NT$17.1 billion, the printed circuit boards manufacturer said.
